name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Por favor, ayúdame a construir un sistema para el análisis de sentimientos en tweets utilizando el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 disponible en Hugging Face. Carga el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 y, al cargar los datos, límpialos eliminando palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa Word2Vec o GloVe para la vectorización de texto. Esto debe ocurrir en `src/data_loader.py`. Luego, implementa y entrena un clasificador SVM en `src/model.py`. Finalmente, escribe un informe de la precisión del clasificador en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Idealmente, el informe debe ser fácilmente interpretable.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.
El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.
Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de descarga del conjunto de datos debe ser confiable, con un manejo claro de errores.
El informe final de precisión debe ser directo y fácil de interpretar.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder that does not actually implement any dataset loading functionality. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any real code to download or load the Sentiment140 dataset from Hugging Face. There is no use of `datasets` library, no `load_dataset("sentiment140")` call, no HTTP requests, or any other mechanism to actually obtain the dataset. The file merely lists the requirement criteria as strings and keywords, confirming it is a stub rather than a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder implementation that does not contain any actual data cleaning logic. It only defines a `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function returning a list of strings. There is no code for removing stop words, punctuation, or special characters. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and does not implement the required preprocessing functionality.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual Word2Vec or GloVe functionality. It is a minimal placeholder that only lists the requirement criteria as strings in a `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` list and includes `"Word2Vec"` and `"GloVe,"` merely as keywords in a `KEYWORDS` list. The `load_dataset()` function returns a dummy dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and there is no actual text vectorization, embedding loading, or any import of libraries such as `gensim`, `torchtext`, or similar that would be needed to use Word2Vec or GloVe embeddings.

judge_stats.criteria: Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement or train an SVM classifier. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder ("model placeholder"). There is no actual SVM implementation (e.g., no `sklearn.svm.SVC`, `LinearSVC`, or any equivalent), no training logic, no feature extraction, and no fitting of a model to data. The file is explicitly described as a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is clearly a placeholder value rather than an actual accuracy score computed from a trained SVM model on the Sentiment140 dataset. There is no evidence in the source files (`data_loader.py`, `model.py`) or trajectory that a real accuracy score was calculated and saved. The file does not contain a meaningful numeric accuracy value.
